conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 20 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 20 years old.

Let me ask you some questions about each child to assess their eligibility.

Starting with Alex (age 8): **Does Alex currently live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me, but I don't actually live in the UK at the moment. We're living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. It's important to know where you're living as location affects eligibility.

**Which country are you and Alex currently living in?** (Child Benefit may still be available if you're living in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant posted abroad.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: We're living in France at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Are you working in France as a Crown servant (for example, for the UK civil service, diplomatic service, or armed forces)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living here as a regular resident.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One more important question about your immigration status: **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you an EEA or Swiss national?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not an EEA or Swiss national.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Now, regarding Blake who is 20 years old: **Is Blake currently in any approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any approved education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either of your children based on your current circumstances:

**For Alex (age 8):**
While Alex is the right age (under 16), Child Benefit requires that you live in the UK. Since you're living in France as a regular resident and are not a Crown servant posted abroad, you do not meet the UK residence requirement. You may only claim Child Benefit while living abroad in limited circumstances, such as being a Crown servant or having certain immigration statuses under the EU Settlement Scheme, which do not apply in your case.

**For Blake (age 20):**
Blake does not qualify because they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit for older children is only available if they are under 20 AND staying in approved education or training.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so would normally meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. However, eligibility requires that you live in the UK. You are currently living in France and are not a Crown servant posted abroad. According to the rules, you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries as a Crown servant, or have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme if you're an EEA or Swiss national. Since you are a regular resident in France without Crown servant status and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, you do not meet the residence requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 or over requires that they are under 20 AND in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, Blake does not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit. The rules state you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training, but Blake does not satisfy these conditions.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 20). While Alex meets the age requirement, the claimant does not meet the UK residence requirement as they are living in France as a regular resident, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

Blake is 20 years old. Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 or over requires that they are under 20 AND in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, Blake does not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit. The rules state you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training, but Blake does not satisfy these conditions.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 20). While Alex meets the age requirement, the claimant does not meet the UK residence requirement as they are living in France as a regular resident, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
